title: BUG: For `infer_dtype` `skipna` is ignored for Period / Interval
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pandas.api.types import infer_dtype

print("interval skipna=True :", infer_dtype([pd.Interval(0, 1), None], skipna=True))
print("interval skipna=False:", infer_dtype([pd.Interval(0, 1), None], skipna=False))

print("period skipna=True   :", infer_dtype([pd.Period("2020Q1"), np.nan], skipna=True))
print("period skipna=False  :", infer_dtype([pd.Period("2020Q1"), np.nan], skipna=False))
```

### Issue Description

`pandas.api.types.infer_dtype` accepts a `skipna` argument, but for `Period` and `Interval` inputs it is currently not applied:

* Interval inference does not pass `skipna` into its validation helper.
* Period inference contains a `FIXME` noting that `skipna` is not actually used.

As a result, `skipna=False` has no effect for these dtypes.

The wiring itself is straightforward, but I’d like to discuss and confirm the desired behavior once `skipna` is honored (e.g. whether the result should become `mixed`, remain `period`/`interval`, or something different? ) before proceeding with an implementation.



### Expected Behavior

`skipna` should actually be honored for `Period` and `Interval` inference. Once we wire it through, I’d expect `skipna=False` to have a real effect when missing values are present (i.e., it shouldn’t behave the same as `skipna=True`).

title: BUG: Plotting with duplicate column names gives misleading error
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [X] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.
- [X] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3], "b": [4, 5, 6]})
df.columns = ["a", "a"]

df.plot.hist()
# TypeError: no numeric data to plot

df.plot.box()
# TypeError: no numeric data to plot
```

### Issue Description

Plotting a DataFrame with duplicate column names raises `TypeError: no numeric data to plot`, even when all columns are numeric.

The root cause is in `MPLPlot.__init__` ([core.py line 193-195](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/blob/main/pandas/plotting/_matplotlib/core.py#L193-L195)):

```python
self.columns = [
    col for col in data.columns if is_numeric_dtype(data[col])
]
```

When columns are non-unique, `data[col]` returns a DataFrame instead of a Series. `is_numeric_dtype` returns `False` for DataFrames, so all duplicate-named columns are silently dropped, leaving `self.columns` empty.

Even if this filter were fixed (e.g. using positional indexing), duplicate column names would still break downstream in multiple places:

- `_ensure_frame` (line ~681): `data.loc[:, cols]` with duplicate names causes column expansion
- `_get_nseries` (line ~507): `len(self.columns)` double-counts duplicate names
- `_compute_plot_data` (line ~691): passes duplicate labels into `reconstruct_data_with_by` → `MultiIndex.from_product`, multiplying the problem
- Scatter/hexbin plots (lines ~1352, 1395): `data[x]`/`data[y]` return DataFrames instead of Series, breaking shape expectations

The plotting pipeline fundamentally relies on label-based indexing (`data[col]`, `data.loc[:, cols]`) at every stage, so duplicate columns can't work without significant rework.

### Expected Behavior

Either:
1. Raise a clear error early (e.g. `ValueError: plotting requires unique column names`), or
2. Handle duplicate columns correctly throughout the pipeline

Option 1 seems most practical given how pervasive the label-based indexing is.

### Installed Versions

<details>
Main branch as of 2026-03-11.
</details>

title: BUG: `DataFrame.any`/`all(skipna=False)` raises ValueError instead of returning NA
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

s = pd.Series([False, None], dtype="boolean")
df = s.to_frame("a")

s.any(skipna=False)
df.any(skipna=False)
```

### Issue Description

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` raise `ValueError: cannot convert float NaN to bool` on numpy-nullable columns (`boolean`, `Int*`, `UInt*`, `Float*`) whenever the reduction would propagate `pd.NA`.

The corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reductions on the same data correctly return `pd.NA`.

### Expected Behavior

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` should return a `Series` with nullable boolean dtype (`boolean`) containing `pd.NA` when the corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reduction on the same column returns `pd.NA`.

title: OPTICS.fit leaks memory when called under VS Code's built-in debugger
body:
### Describe the bug

Running clustering algorithm with n_jobs parameter set to more than 1 thread causes memory leak each time algorithm is run.
This simple code causes additional memory leak at each loop cycle. The issue will not occur if i replace manifold reduction algorithm with precomputed features.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import gc
import numpy as np
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
from sklearn.cluster import OPTICS
import psutil
process = psutil.Process()


def main():
    data = np.random.random((100, 100))
    for _i in range(1, 50):
        points = TSNE().fit_transform(data)
        prediction = OPTICS(n_jobs=2).fit_predict(points)  # n_jobs!=1
        points = None
        prediction = None
        del prediction
        del points
        gc.collect()
        print(f"{process.memory_info().rss / 1e6:.1f} MB")


main()
```

### Expected Results

Program's memory usage nearly constant between loop cycles

### Actual Results

Program's memory usage increases infinitely

title: `_safe_indexing` fails with pyarrow==16.0.0

I found apache/arrow#42013 addressing this and it was fixed for version 17.0.0. Upgrading my pyarrow version has resolved the issue for me.

In the CI, we test in `pylatest_conda_forge_mkl_linux` with pyarrow==20.0.0 (only).

title: BUG `RidgeCV` with `gcv_mode="svd"` is unstable when alpha is very small
body:
### Describe the bug

As observed in this PR https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/pull/27842#discussion_r2361459678 there is a bug with `RidgeCV`with `gcv_mode="svd"`. The problem seems to appear for very small alpha values (1e-14 or less). The other `gcv_mode="eigen"` works fine.

It's maybe related to this bug https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/26848#issuecomment-1638986852 where `alpha` is not very small but fitting is done with large `sample_weight`, again `gcv_mode="eigen"` works fine but `gcv_mode="svd"` gives diverging coefficients and very poor score.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression
from sklearn.linear_model import RidgeCV
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

alphas = [1e-15, 1e-14]
X, y = make_regression(n_samples=1000, n_features=50, noise=0, random_state=42)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.9, random_state=42)
n_train = X_train.shape[0]
ridge_cv = RidgeCV(alphas=alphas, gcv_mode="svd")
ridge_cv.fit(X_train, y_train)
score = ridge_cv.score(X_test, y_test)
min_coef = ridge_cv.coef_.min()
max_coef = ridge_cv.coef_.max()
print(f"{score=:.2f} {min_coef=:.2f} {max_coef=:.2f}")
```

### Expected Results

We expect an also perfect fit in this case, eg `gcv_mode="eigen"` gives
```python
# score=1.00 min_coef=-0.00 max_coef=94.78
```

### Actual Results

The score is catastrophic and there is an issue with the fitted coefficients magnitude.
```python
# score=-37754.60 min_coef=-9221.16 max_coef=11981.91
```

### Versions

```shell
sklearn: 1.8.dev0
```

title: Using `BisectingKMeans` with `init` raises a possibly dubious `ValueError`
body:
When using `BisectingKMeans`, `n_clusters` greater than 2, using `init` to specify cluster seeds.  A `ValueError` is raised if returning fewer than `n_clusters` seeds, but the value of `n_clusters` passed to `init` is (correctly, for bisecting k-means) always 2.  The size of the returned object *must* match the "outer" `n_clusters` to avoid a `ValueError`, and changing the other cluster seeds gives a different clustering result.

In essence: is it intended behaviour for a *bisecting* method to require more than 2 initial cluster seeds per bisection, and if so, why does it change the result?  Why do some seed orderings not change the result, and some do?  (My basic testing is not exhaustive.)


------


In my case, I was running bisecting k-means clustering on image data. Hence, in my examples below I will use Scikit-Image data for similarity.

For example, this fails:

<details>

  <summary>Calling `init` causes a panic</summary>

  ```python
  import numpy as np
  import skimage as ski
  from sklearn.cluster import BisectingKMeans


  def bk_init(X, n_clusters, random_state):
      ## note this is always 2:
      print(n_clusters)

      return np.array(
          [[0], [len(X)]],
          dtype=np.uint,
      )


  k = 6

  img   = ski.data.camera()
  img_v = img.ravel()
  img_d = img_v.reshape(-1, 1)

  bk = BisectingKMeans(n_clusters=k, init=bk_init)
  bk.fit(img_d)
  ```

</details>

with a `ValueError`:

<details>

  <summary>`init` -> `ValueError` traceback</summary>

  ```
  Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "<python-input-5>", line 2, in <module>
      bk.fit(img_d)
      ~~~~~~^^^^^^^
    File "/<masked>/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/sklearn/base.py", line 1336, in wrapper
      return fit_method(estimator, *args, **kwargs)
    File "/<masked>/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/sklearn/cluster/_bisect_k_means.py", line 430, in fit
      self._bisect(X, x_squared_norms, sample_weight, cluster_to_bisect)
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    File "/<masked>/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/sklearn/cluster/_bisect_k_means.py", line 322, in _bisect
      centers_init = self._init_centroids(
          X,
      ...<4 lines>...
          sample_weight=sample_weight,
      )
    File "/<masked>/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/sklearn/cluster/_kmeans.py", line 1040, in _init_centroids
      self._validate_center_shape(X, centers)
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^
    File "/<masked>/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/sklearn/cluster/_kmeans.py", line 939, in _validate_center_shape
      raise ValueError(
      ...<2 lines>...
      )
  ValueError: The shape of the initial centers (2, 1) does not match the number of clusters 6.
  ```

</details>

A work-around is to encapsulate the "outer" `n_clusters` in the `init` function:

<details>

  <summary>Capturing outer `n_clusters`</summary>

  ```python
  import numpy as np
  import skimage as ski
  from sklearn.cluster import BisectingKMeans


  k = 6


  def bk_init(X, n_clusters, random_state):
      ## this is still 2:
      print(n_clusters)

      i = np.zeros(
          (k, 1),         # NOTE not ``n_clusters``!
          dtype=np.uint,
      )

      i[0], i[1] = (0, len(X))

      return i


  img   = ski.data.camera()
  img_v = img.ravel()
  img_d = img_v.reshape(-1, 1)

  bk = BisectingKMeans(n_clusters=k, init=bk_init)
  bk.fit(img_d)
  ```

</details>

Also see [`_BisectingTree.split`][bts], which appears to show getting indices 0 and 1 only (but I'm probably misinterpreting it).  But clearly something else is at play, because changing the ordering of the remaining indices changes the result **in some, but not all, cases** (though this doesn't cover every permutation):

<details>

  <summary>Different results from different seeds</summary>

  ```python
  print(bk.cluster_centers_)  # previous result
  ```

  ```
  [[156.        ]
   [159.        ]
   [174.86169245]
   [ 12.        ]
   [ 10.        ]
   [  0.5       ]]
  ```

  ```python
  def bk_init(X, n_clusters, random_state):
      return np.array(
          ## different results to the below and to each other:
          # [[0], [64],    [729], [5555],   [1], [len(X)]],
          # [[0],  [1], [len(X)],   [64], [729],   [5555]],
          ## identical results (yes, even the first two):
          # [[0],    [729], [5555],      [1], [len(X)],   [64]],
          # [[0],   [5555],    [1], [len(X)],     [64],  [729]],
          # [[0], [len(X)],   [64],    [729],   [5555],    [1]],
          # [[0], [len(X)],  [729],   [5555],      [1],   [64]],
          # [[0], [len(X)], [5555],      [1],     [64],  [729]],
          # [[0], [len(X)],    [1],     [64],    [729], [5555]],
          [[0], [len(X)], [64], [729], [5555], [1]],
          dtype=np.uint,
      )


  bk = BisectingKMeans(n_clusters=k, init=bk_init)
  bk.fit(img_d)
  print(bk.cluster_centers_)
  ```

  ```
  [[127.49822064]
   [126.        ]
   [116.        ]
   [112.        ]
   [ 11.96384212]
   [  0.5       ]]
  ```

</details>

(I'd like to run a test using `itertools.permutation`.)


[bts]: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/c5497b7f7eacfaff061cf68e09bcd48aa93d4d6b/sklearn/cluster/_bisect_k_means.py#L46


------


<details>

  <summary>Python and package versions</summary>

  - `uv version`: `uv 0.9.26 (Homebrew 2026-01-15)`
  - `uv run python --version`: `Python 3.13.11`
  - `pyproject.toml`, dependencies:
    + `matplotlib>=3.10.8`
    + `multiprocess>=0.70.18`

title: Wrong chance_level plot in PrecisionRecallDisplay when using Pytorch data
body: ### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact The chance_level line drawn by `PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions` when `plot_chance_level=True` is not correct when `y_true` is an instance of `pytorch.Tensor`. ### Steps/Code to Reproduce ```python # pytorch==2.8.0 from sklearn.metrics import PrecisionRecallDisplay import torch torch.manual_seed(42) y_true = torch.cat([torch.ones(40), torch.zeros(60)]) # Chance level equal 0.4 y_score = torch.rand_like(y_true) PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions(y_true, y_score, plot_chance_level=True) # Wrong PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions(y_true.numpy(), y_score, plot_chance_level=True) # Correct ``` ### Expected Results Correct chance classifier line at y 0.4 with y_true as a numpy array. ### Actual Results Wrong chance classifier line at y 0.0 with y_true as a pytorch tensor. ### Versions ```shell System: python: 3.13.5 | packaged by Anaconda, Inc. | (main, Jun 12 2025, 16:37:03) [MSC v.1929 64 bit (AMD64)] executable: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\python.exe machine: Windows-11-10.0.26200-SP0 Python dependencies: sklearn: 1.7.1 pip: 25.1 setuptools: 78.1.1 numpy: 2.1.2 scipy: 1.16.1 Cython: None pandas: 2.3.3 matplotlib: 3.10.5 joblib: 1.5.1 threadpoolctl: 3.6.0 Built with OpenMP: True threadpoolctl info: user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 6 prefix: libiomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\torch\lib\libiomp5md.dll version: None user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 1 prefix: libiomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\torch\lib\libiompstubs5md.dll version: None user_api: blas internal_api: openblas num_threads: 12 prefix: libscipy_openblas filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\numpy.libs\libscipy_openblas64_-c16e4918366c6bc1f1cd71e28ca36fc0.dll version: 0.3.27 threading_layer: pthreads architecture: Haswell user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 12 prefix: vcomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\sklearn\.libs\vcomp140.dll version: None user_api: blas internal_api: openblas num_threads: 12 prefix: libscipy_openblas filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\scipy.libs\libscipy_openblas-6b2103f2ae4d8547998b5d188e9801fb.dll version: 0.3.28 threading_layer: pthreads architecture: Haswell ``` ### Interest in fixing the bug Working on this, the root cause is due to these reasons: - pytorch (and potentially other array libraries) defines scalars in such a way that their hash is different from their value and same values may have different hashes; - `PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions` relies on `Counter` class to compare `y_true` entries with `pos_label` values in order to generate `prevalence_pos_label` (chance level). https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/646da0f072a8afef6a980aa427a710311e67eb9d/sklearn/metrics/_plot/precision_recall_curve.py#L563-L565 - `Counter` is not able to group objects with same values since they have different hash values. Comparing the values from `y_true` directly to `pos_label` to calculate `prevalence_pos_label` should be enough to fix this.

title: Issue w/ latest nightlies: "C function scipy.linalg.cython_blas.dasum has wrong signature"
body:
### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact

The latest `scikit-learn` and `scipy` nightlies are incompatible with each other. Trying to import from `scikit-learn` in an environment where they're installed results in an error like this:

> TypeError: C function scipy.linalg.cython_blas.dasum has wrong signature (expected __pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_d (int *, __pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_d *, int *), got __pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_d (__pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_blas_int *, __pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_d *, __pyx_t_5scipy_6linalg_11cython_blas_blas_int *))

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

Ran the following today on an aarch64 system:

```shell
docker run \
    --rm \
    -it python:3.13 \
    bash

pip install \
    --prefer-binary \
    --extra-index-url https://pypi.anaconda.org/scientific-python-nightly-wheels/simple \
    'scikit-learn>=1.9.dev0' \
    'scipy>=1.18.dev0'
```

Just importing `sklearn.metrics.cluster` was enough to trigger the issue


### Expected Results

Imports like the following should succeed:

```python
from sklearn.model_selection import BaseCrossValidator, GroupKFold, StratifiedKFold
```

This is taken from the stack trace of the LightGBM CI job that caught this: https://github.com/lightgbm-org/LightGBM/actions/runs/23438762347/job/68214781982?pr=7167

Reproducible example above uses  `from sklearn.metrics import cluster` because that seems like the closest public-API import to the bug.

### Actual Results

Those imports fail with the error reported above.

### Versions

```shell

threadpoolctl==3.6.0
```

### Interest in fixing the bug

No, sorry, and I do not have an idea of how to fix it right now.

It looks like something similar happened about 2 years ago, and the root cause ended up being a change in `scipy`:

* https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/26290
* https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/18377

title: HTML display parameter/attribute links don't work with ` in the docstring type
body: ### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact If parameters or fitted attributes contain ` in their docstring type description, linking to the specific entry does not work. ### Steps/Code to Reproduce Clicking on `feature_names_in_` in the HTML representation of [this example](https://scikit-learn.org/dev/auto_examples/applications/plot_cyclical_feature_engineering.html#gradient-boosting) should open the docs at the specific entry (as it does for all other parameters and attributes), but the docstring contains ``` `n_features_in_` ``` and this seems to make the link to fall back to the top of the page instead. When removing the ` around it, the link works as expected. ### Expected Results Being directed to ### Actual Results Goes to the top of the page instead ### Versions ```shell sklearn 1.9.dev0 ``` ### Interest in fixing the bug @DeaMariaLeon, could you please look into this?

title: Using dask distributed with a pipeline and column_transformer breaks metadata routing
body:
### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact

when using a pipeline with a columns transformer, using the pipeline in a distributed environment seems to raise a strange error.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.compose import make_column_transformer
from sklearn.pipeline import make_pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, QuantileTransformer
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score

X, y = make_classification()

pipeline = make_pipeline(
    make_column_transformer(
        (StandardScaler(), [0]),
    ),
    # StandardScaler(),
    RandomForestClassifier(
        max_depth=7, n_estimators=300, random_state=42
    )
)
pipeline

cv_results = cross_val_score(pipeline, X, y, cv=5, n_jobs=1)
print("CV score:", np.mean(cv_results))

from distributed import Client
from joblib import parallel_config

client = Client()

with parallel_config("dask"):
    cv_scores = cross_val_score(pipeline, X, y, cv=5, n_jobs=-1)

print("CV score:", np.mean(cv_scores))
client.close()
```

title: Delete Cell button is not accessible using arrow key navigation in notebook toolbar:A11y_Visual Studio Code Jupyter Extensions_Home_Keyboard
body:
## GitHub Tags
#A11yUsable; #A11yTCS; #Win32;#DesktopApp; #Visual Studio Code Jupyter Extensions; #GH-VSCodeJupyterExtensions-Win32-Oct2025; #FTP; #Keyboard; #SCIM:Bug5313479;

## Environment Details:
Application: Visual Studio Code Jupyter Extensions
Version: 1.105.0-insider (user setup)
OS build :26100.4770

## Repro Steps
Launch the Visual studio code > Install Jupyter extension app > Open Jupyter Notebook > Delete button >Observe the issue

## Actual Experience.
The “Delete Cell (D D)” button is focusable, but it is not reachable using arrow key navigation within the notebook toolbar.
Users must press the Tab key separately to move focus to the Delete button.
Note: Issue repro in MAC also

## Expected Experience
The Delete Cell button should be reachable using arrow key navigation (Left/Right Arrow) along with other adjacent toolbar controls, without requiring a separate Tab key interaction.

## User Impact:

- Keyboard-only users must switch between arrow keys and Tab navigation, causing inconsistency.
- Breaks expected navigation pattern within the same toolbar group.
- Reduces efficiency and increases effort for users relying solely on keyboard interaction.

title: Screen reader announces incorrect numeric position for list items in Permission Picker combobox:A11y_VisualStudioCode_Permissions levels in Copilot CLI_ScreenReader
body:
## GitHub Tags
#A11yTCS;#Visual Studio Code Client; #SH_Integrated browser features_Win32_JUNE2026;#DesktopApp;#FTP;#Win32;#A11ySev2;#A11yMAS;#WCAG1.3.1;  #ScreenReader; #NVDA;

## Issue Subcategory 
List items not in a list container 

## User Experience 
Screen reader users cannot identify content as a list or hear the total item count. List navigation shortcuts (e.g., “L” key in JAWS) will not work. 

## Repro Steps 
Turn on NVDA screen reader > Open VS Code > Copilot chat > Permission Picker combo box > Observe the issue: Screen reader announces incorrect numeric position for list items in Permission Picker combobox

## Actual Result 
In the Permission Picker combo box, there are a total of 4 list items. However, while navigating using a screen reader, focus does not move to the 4th list item. After the 3rd item, the focus visually moves but the screen reader announces incorrect positional information such as “5 of 5”, even though only 4 items are present.

## Expected Result 
Screen reader focus should move sequentially to all list items, including the 4th item, and announce the correct positional information

## How to Fix 
Wrap list items in ul (unordered), ol (ordered), or dl (description) with the correct child elements (li, dt/dd). Alternatively, use ARIA roles such as role="list" and role="listitem" to remediate non-list markup as needed. 

## Environment details 
Application Name: VS Code 
VS Code version: 1.124.2
Edition Windows 11 Enterprise
Version 25H2
OS build 26200.8390
NVDA: 2025.3.3

## Note

- Please do not close this bug. This bug should only be closed by TCS, C+AI Accessibility tester after bug verification.

title: Screen reader announces redundant/unnecessary link information in Enable Bypass Approvals dialog:A11y_VisualStudioCode_Permissions levels in Copilot CLI_ScreenReader
body:
## GitHub Tags
#A11yTCS;#Visual Studio Code Client; #SH_Integrated browser features_Win32_JUNE2026;#DesktopApp;#FTP;#Win32;#A11ySev2;#A11yMAS;#WCAG1.3.1;  #ScreenReader; #NVDA;

## Issue Subcategory 
Supplementary information is not programmatically associated 

## User Experience 
Screen reader users may not hear important helper text, requirements, or error context associated with a form field, so they may not understand how to complete it correctly.

## Repro Steps 
Turn on NVDA screen reader > Open VS Code > Copilot chat > Permission Picker combo box > Bypass approvals > “chat.permissions.default" link > Observe the issue: Screen reader announces redundant/unnecessary link information in Permission Picker dialog

## Actual Result 
After announcing the link name and role (e.g., “chat.permissions.default, link”), the screen reader further announces unnecessary information such as internal command text (e.g., “command:workbench.action.openSettings…”), which is not meaningful to users.

## Expected Result 
Screen reader should announce only meaningful and user-relevant information (link name and role). Internal or technical details (e.g., command paths or IDs) should not be exposed to assistive technologies.

## How to Fix 
 Use aria-describedby to reference visible helper or error text, or use aria-description when appropriate for concise descriptive text that is not otherwise visible. 

## Environment details 
Application Name: VS Code 
VS Code version: 1.124.2
Edition Windows 11 Enterprise
Version 25H2
OS build 26200.8390

title: Title bar menu overlaps window control buttons
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes * VS Code Version: 1.124.2 (System Setup) * OS Version: Windows 11 24H2 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Set the following settings: ```json "workbench.activityBar.location": "top", "workbench.activityBar.compact": true ``` 2. Reload VS Code. 3. Right-click the application icon in the top-left corner of the window. 4. Observe the position of the system context menu. Expected Result: The system context menu should be fully visible and should not overlap the window control buttons. Actual Result: The system context menu is positioned too high and visually overlaps the minimize, maximize, and close button area. Part of the menu appears underneath the title bar controls, resulting in a misaligned appearance. Notes: The issue appears when the Activity Bar is moved to the top and compact mode is enabled. The problem seems to be purely visual and does not affect functionality, but it reduces the overall visual polish and consistency of the interface. Screenshots attached.

title: Visual Studio Code 1.126: explorer.incrementalNaming (simple) stopped working
body:
Type: Bug After Visual Studio Code 1.126 updated: 1. Copy a file in Explorer pane (CTRL+C or mouse right-click > context menu > copy). 2. Paste file in same folder or other location. 3. Expected (explorer.incrementalNaming: simple): copy of file is created with 'copy' appended to filename. 4. Issue: confirm prompt appears and when OK button clicked no copy is created -- instead a notification appears with message: "The file(s) to paste have been deleted or moved since you copied them. Unable to move/copy... ". 5. Attempted: changed explorer.incrementalNaming to smart, restart vscode -- did not fix. Changed setting back to simple, restart vscode -- did not fix. 6. Update: described issue has gone away about an hour after returning from break. VS Code version: Code 1.126.0 (7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a, 2026-06-23T01:46:13Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2

title: BYOK CustomEndpoint Model only thinks, cannot parse or strip `delta.reasoning`
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: GitHub.copilot-chat 0.41 - VS Code Version: 1.126.0 - OS Version: Win 11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): all responses - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): CustomEndpoint (Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking-FP8) Steps to Reproduce: 1. Assign a reasoning model as a custom endpoint. 2. Ask it to do or say anything. 3. Click on the "Finished in.." section when you receive the error to see the thinking. This does not happen with qwen3.6-27b, which can "think" and "respond" as normal. It seems to be that the extension does not expect and cannot handle `delta.reasoning`.
